meta.title: The Sanctuary | Exhibition Booth Build | Construction Portfolio
meta.description: Case study of The Sanctuary, an exhibition booth build focused on curved architectural forms, premium finishes, and coordinated on-site execution.
meta.ogDescription: From wood framing and MDF cladding to finish-ready detailing, The Sanctuary booth highlights disciplined execution for trade show environments.

hero.title: The Sanctuary
hero.summary: A premium booth build that transformed a concept into an immersive, sculpted, and highly functional exhibition environment.

role.label: Scope
role.value: Wood frame structure, MDF cladding for flat and curved surfaces, finish-ready detailing, electrical/lighting coordination, and onsite installation support.

details.title: Project Details
details.body: The Sanctuary scope covered end-to-end technical delivery from wood frame structure through final-ready substrate preparation. Work included MDF panel coverage on both flat and curved surfaces, with tight geometry control to achieve clean reveals and joints ready for finishing. Field coordination ensured lighting and electrical readiness at interface points before close-up, and onsite install support kept sequencing, fit-up, and quality aligned with project milestones.

overview.title: Overview
overview.body: The objective was to translate a branded concept into an inviting exhibition booth that guided visitor flow and showcased key touchpoints. The design language prioritized soft transitions, curated textures, and balanced lighting for a premium presentation.

challenge.title: Challenge
challenge.body: The build required precise alignment across curved geometries, ceiling transitions, and multiple finishing trades within tight move-in windows, without compromising visual continuity on the show floor.

approach.title: Approach
approach.body: Execution followed coordinated booth-delivery phases: prefabrication planning, framing and substrate installation, progressive finishing, MEP readiness checks, and final on-site fit-up reviews. Daily communication kept sequencing predictable and quality consistent through handover.

highlights.title: Highlights
items: Primary build system: wood frame structure
MDF panel cladding across flat and curved surfaces
Finish-ready surfaces with clean reveals and precise joints
Coordination for lighting and electrical readiness before closure

credits.title: Credits
credits.body: Construction execution and site coordination by the project team in collaboration with client-selected design and finishing partners.

cta.title: Need a similar execution on your project?
cta.body: Let’s discuss your scope, schedule, and finish requirements.
